requirement: Extract EPC distribution (energy efficiency rating bands A-G) by area — identify MEES exposure risk

sources_used.source: DLUHC Energy Performance of Buildings Certificates — Live Table D1
sources_used.data: Domestic properties by Local Authority by Energy Efficiency Rating (A/B/C/D/E/F/G), quarterly 2008-Q4 to 2025-Q4
sources_used.result: SUCCESS — 23,149 rows, all 5 target LAs present

data_now_in_macro_trend.epc_distribution: written to data/macro_trend.json as top-level key 'epc_distribution'

coverage.london_wide: quarterly 2008/4 to 2025/4 (18 years, 18 data points at Q4)
coverage.Belsize_Park_NW3: Camden LA — quarterly history + current snapshot
coverage.West_Hampstead_NW6: Brent LA — quarterly history + current snapshot
coverage.Islington_N1: Islington LA — quarterly history + current snapshot
coverage.Chelsea_SW3_SW10: Kensington and Chelsea LA — quarterly history + current snapshot

london_wide_current_2025q4.trend_from_2008: EPC stock dramatically improved: 2008 had 5.6% F/G exposure vs 0.6% today

Belsize_Park_NW3.note: Higher E band (6.0%) vs peers — Georgian/regency stock

West_Hampstead_NW6.note: Best avg score of all 5 areas

Chelsea_SW3_SW10.note: Highest C+ proportion — period conversions good

Bayswater_W2.note: Westminster LA not in D1 Sheet 7 — Bayswater W2 EPC derived from Westminster LA data

key_findings.mees_risk_summary: All 5 target areas have <1% F/G exposure. MEES 2028 compliance risk is negligible for all target areas. Chelsea and Islington have the cleanest stock.

acquisition_implications.low_mees_risk: No F/G properties in target areas — no forced retrofit pressure on acquisition
acquisition_implications.E_band_properties: Belsize Park (NW3) has highest E-band (6.0%) — potential negotiation lever if EPC not yet upgraded
